main_topic: How to start a profitable online coaching business quickly using a simple Instagram funnel and minimal systems.
transcript: My name is Zach Pererna and I have been online coaching for 7 years. I've made upwards of $3 million coaching and now I help others do the same. Today I'm going to give a few strategies on how to get started. Most people spend so long overthinking and wasting time and just delaying this. It's crazy. If you implement what I'm about to teach you today, you can probably make uh quite a bit of money, upwards of probably 5 to 10 grand in your first month. So, by the end of today's video, you're going to know exactly how to get started with your online coaching, and you should be pretty excited about it. Now, for those who don't know, my name is Zach Per, and I have been online coaching for 7 years. have made upwards of $3 million coaching and now I help others do the same. I don't help them make 3 million but I mean if they want to but I help them make money online coaching. So today I'm going to give a few strategies from the social blueprint on what I teach my students on how to get started. So getting straight into it, this is going to be perfect for you if maybe you're a personal trainer wanting to go online but you've never really known how or needed the systems in place or you had a lot of questions. Maybe you just love fitness and you've helped a lot of people before in fitness but uh you'd love to do it for a job. Now, getting straight into it, but before we go any further, I just want to give a bit of context on a bit of a a model that I use in my coaching program. And it's the attract, convert, and deliver. And what we want to do is basically nail all of these things so that we can create something that's pretty special for people. Because if we can attract an audience, get eyeballs, and we can convert them and make money, and we can deliver a good service, which is changing people's lives and meaning, we get this nice sweet spot in the middle, which is uh a successful business. Now, at any given time, you need to focus on one of these things, right? And sometimes as a business owner, we're kind of doing them all. But what I would like you guys to focus on is we're going to do one at a time. So as we talk today, I'm going to give you six steps and it's essentially going to go through on setting up the business and what you need, how to get people to look at your stuff, and then how to sell them on it. So I'm going to give you six steps. The first step is to pick your promise. Your promise is essentially what you're offering to the market. So this is creating your offer. Who you going to help? What do they want to get out of it? What is your niche? That kind of stuff. Now, if you haven't heard before, niching down is the basically the term used to describe choosing a target market and going all in on them and excluding a lot of other people. So, typically when I tell people to niche down, they usually say the same thing of, well, I don't want to exclude certain people. And a bit of a reframe is that we need to exclude certain people, especially now that it's getting competitive and there's people in the market because if we can exclude certain people, it means that we're going to speak more closely to another specific type of person. So, if I say, for example, I only help moms get in shape, all of a sudden I'm going to exclude all the dads and all the young guys. But with that comes a level of superiority, I guess, or at least expert in that subject matter because then the moms might look at me and go, "Oh, he only targets moms. I want to work with someone who gets me." So, it implies a level of understanding that is really specific to that individual group that maybe no one else has. Now, whether that's true or not is kind of beside the point, but it still implies it. So, it it helps for marketing and sales. Now, obviously the the more specific you work with a group, the better you are going to be anyway at at dealing with those problems. So, I highly recommend niching down. But the problem in the beginning that not many people tell you about is that when you go to niche down, either you're doing it because you were told to, so you choose some random niche, or you uh dive into one without really thinking it through. So, I want to give you a couple of uh of things you can do. One, if you're going to choose your niche, think about who you could help, who you want to help, and what kind of content you want to make as well. Because if you're going to niche down, you're going to have to make content for that niche. If you don't know any of that, and you think, "Well, I'm just starting. How am I supposed to know? Also, totally fine. This is what I tell my guys is to start relatively broad in this case because in the beginning, we need to use whoever we've got. We need to use our friends, family, Instagram followers, everyone. And granted, they're probably not moms following on Instagram if that's going to be your target market. If you're going to I'm going to choose busy dads and you're like a 25-year-old guy who has like one dad following him and it's yours. So, like you want to be realistic in the beginning and go let's get experience, let's make some money, why not? Instead of I'm going to set this up and just go all in on the niche. So, I think if you don't know, it's cool to start broad, and that's okay. And we're going to refine it over time. What I like to do when refining people's niches is look at who's resonating with their content, who's responding to their stuff, who's booking sales calls, who's actually becoming a client, and then making extrapolations out of that and going, "Okay, I'm going to target those people because for whatever reason, I resonate a little bit more with them and they resonate better with me." So, the first one is choosing the niche and choosing what problems you're going to solve. Now, muscle gain or fat loss typically or a holistic approach, right? That's the whole thing that you need to decide on what you really want to specialize in and what you want to bring to the table. When in doubt, fat loss is very easy just to go, "Yep, I'm going to go with fat loss. That's my thing." Because everyone wants to lose fat. Most people do. We are probably the fattest we have ever been in the history of the world right now despite being having a lot more coaching opportunities and access to to healthy food, but we are still getting fatter as a as a nation. So, and actually as probably as a planet for the most part. So, I don't think that niche is going anywhere for a while. So, when in doubt, I would go fat loss. Now, number two is to create your offer. So your offer basically has three things. The first one is training. Now with training, it's it's typically done through uh any one of the softwares. You've got trainer eyes, kahunas, onefitit, ever fit. There's a lot. They're all good. It doesn't really matter. Trainer eyes is probably the easiest and the most commonly used. So I would just go with whatever one you want to do, but you're going to give them a training plan. And then there's nutrition. Now you're either going to give uh like meal examples is is in a meal plan or macros. Now these are all recommendations. Remember cuz nutritionists can give actual meal plans. that that's the qualification in like actual meal plans, eat this. But in online coaching, it's never really eat this. It's uh here's what you can do, here's what you can eat, here's a suggestion, right? Consult with your nutritionist before following this obviously. So you can have disclaimers and things like that to protect yourself and the person too, obviously. But if you are not qualified and you I mean personal trainers aren't qualified to give a meal plan either. So if you're doing this, you can either do macros or you can do a meal plan. And finally, you're going to have uh accountability, which is basically just support, right? So you can have all the information in the world, but the accountability and the support is going to be the difference between going at it yourself and struggling for a while versus implementing and just skyrocketing ahead. So accountability is really uh huge and it's really what people pay for. So in kind of coming up with all of these things, we're going to price this as our main offer. Now, in terms of pricing, I like to do a really simple approach. Let's just say you're choosing 12 weeks or 16 weeks or whatever you'd like to do. And you want to have it for a painful price, which is going to be the cheapest as the most discounted. So say 1,200 for 12 weeks. or you're going to have a weekly price if they wanted to go installments which is going to be slightly more expensive. So around 120 a week versus 1,200. So if you chose this one, you're saving basically an extra couple hundred bucks basically. So that's a really easy way to get started and you can you can charge whatever you want. But what I would like suggest is don't charge less than uh $80 a week really cuz most people just stick just charge at 80. But don't charge less than 80 cuz you're going to want to give it your absolute best. Um and you're going to want to make it worth your while. So if you game, go a 24- week and go even more expensive. Now, aside from this, we don't want to complicate it with having too many systems because most people think, "Oh, I need a website. I need everything ready to go. I've got to have this complicated funnel here and all this." And it's like, you really don't need any of that stuff until you're absolutely smashing it. In the beginning, you want things to be simple as when I first started, I had a spreadsheet I made by myself and it was horribly disgusting, but it worked. It got the job done. I had just messaging people. I had calls that I would like literally just give them a phone call. Now there there's a few softwares are going to make life a little bit easier but that's beside the point like you could literally get started and launch with this idea of your offer. If we take a zoomed in approach here, there's going to be uh training, which is going to be workout program, let's say, nutrition, so macros or a meal plan variation. And accountability is going to be through check-in, some kind of process like that, like your aoom check-in or a Zoom check-in where you actually have a face tof face conversation or a phone call or even just text support or a combination of everything. So, it's just a way to keep people accountable and make sure they get the results. Now, step three is to use Instagram as your funnel. So, when you're just starting, you need to leverage your entire network. So, you need to leverage your social media. This means posting on your personal brand, on your personal account. I don't want you to make a brand new fitness account that no one cares about, no one follows cuz you're starting from zero. In the beginning, you need traction. You need to put yourself out there and leverage your personal account. So, I did this and I recommend all of my guys do this as well. And what you can do is essentially start putting it out there that this is what you're doing, that you're taking on some clients and and you're you're going to be posting about fitness. What you can do to make the most of this is pop up a story and put up one story with text on screen. Maybe a transformation of yourself if you haven't had a client yet and say, "I'm just starting my online coaching. I'm looking for five, you know, people or niche or whatever that want to, you know, lose 5 kilos or get in their best shape in the next 12 weeks. If you're interested, drop a message below or you just put a poll in there and they can vote on the poll. And now you DM those people and you have a conversation and you see what their problems are, see if they need help and you can escalate that to a call. And on the call, you can present your program and you can talk about it. You can help them out and you can kind of go from there. So, that's a simple Instagram funnel. And if we look at what it actually is, it's doing a couple of things. It's putting a CTA out, which is a call to action. Then, it's encouraging someone to to do something. That's taking an action. So, they're going to take an action. They're going to vote on your story as a poll or message you or whatever that is. And once they uh do that, you're going to have a conversation in the DMs. This is where you're going to probe a little bit further and go, "Oh, you know, what have you been dealing with? What kind of goals do you have? What have you tried before? What's the problems?" And then once they tell you a little bit more about that, you're going to pitch a call. You're not going to discuss the price in the DMs because then people will value it based on the price and not really based on what they're going to get or what the result is, which is the most important thing. So, in the DMs, you pitch the call just to get a basically a further understanding of of how you can help them, further understanding their problems. And then on the call, you'll talk about them way more than you. You'll talk about them for 30 minutes. Go into the details of how they need help and what the problem is and what they would do for them if they fixed it. Just so you can get a really good understanding of how important this is to them as well. And then after that, you'll pitch your program. It's as simple as that. So, you'll notice here there is no website. There's no website in this whole in this whole mix. It's strictly an Instagram funnel. That's why it's really easy and it gets the job done. Now, I want to pause right here just really quickly and uh let you guys know that if you don't want to do this yourself and you want to do it with me and let me kind of like show you exactly how to do it and look at your work and go, yeah, move on. That's great. Then book a call below and chat to the team. So, when it comes to stacking results, we need to post our social proof all the time. slides.title: Stop Overthinking — Start Now
slides.body: Follow a simple step plan and make $5–10K month one.

slides.title: Perfectionism Kills Momentum
slides.body: Overcomplicating and building every system kills traction.

slides.title: One Stage, Big Results
slides.body: Choose one stage: Attract, Convert, or Deliver. Master it first.

slides.title: Minimum Viable Offer
slides.body: Training, nutrition guidance, and accountability — your MVP offer.

slides.title: Close the sale
slides.body: Pitch benefits, handle objections, then send Stripe link to complete payment.

slides.title: Take Action Today
slides.body: Post one story offering to take clients now. Or book a Social Blueprint call.

caption: Stop overthinking and follow one simple plan to hit your first $5–10K month. This carousel teaches one-stage focus, an MVP offer (training + nutrition + accountability), and the exact steps to pitch and close.
